title: POW_DIFFICULTY invalid parse silently disables PoW
description: main.rs:109-112 — POW_DIFFICULTY is parsed with .parse::<u8>().ok().unwrap_or(0). If the value is invalid (e.g. '20x', 'off', '300' which overflows u8), parse fails silently and 0 is used, disabling PoW entirely with no log warning. All other env vars (KEEPALIVE_SECS, RATE_LIMIT_MSG_PER_MIN, SUBSCRIPTION_DAYS) explicitly warn on parse failure and use a safe default. POW_DIFFICULTY is a security gate; silently disabling it when misconfigured is a footgun. An operator who sets POW_DIFFICULTY=20x gets no error message and no PoW gate. Fix: match the pattern used for KEEPALIVE_SECS (explicit warn\! on parse failure) or use anyhow::bail\! to refuse startup on invalid input.
